title: Causal Discovery in Time-Series
recommendation: Use a gradient-boosted or forest regressor with lagged traffic and weather features when the goal is traffic disruption analysis, and treat Granger significance as supportive evidence rather than definitive causal proof.

title: Conformal Prediction for Supply Chains
recommendation: For inventory planning on thin daily demand data, start with split conformal intervals around a tree ensemble because the coverage is easy to calibrate and usually narrower than naive safety-stock rules.

title: Explainable Boosting Machines in High-Stakes Domains
recommendation: Choose an EBM when you need near-ensemble accuracy with feature-level accountability. If a black-box model only wins marginally, the interpretability trade-off usually favors the EBM in regulated credit workflows.

title: Handling Concept Drift in Streaming Data
recommendation: If your label boundary shifts over time, favor incremental learners with explicit update loops over a frozen batch model, even when the initial batch score looks strong.

title: Federated Learning on Extreme Low-Bandwidth
recommendation: Use top-k or sign-compressed federated updates when uplink budget is the constraint. Full-precision FedAvg is only justified when the last few accuracy points matter more than transmission cost.

title: Tabular Meta-Learning
recommendation: If you are benchmarking tiny tabular datasets, store meta-features and prior model rankings. Even a simple nearest-dataset recommender can cut the search space before full hyperparameter tuning.

title: Synthetic Tabular Data Privacy
recommendation: Use copula-style synthesis when you need stronger utility, and move toward noisier histogram-style methods when privacy risk dominates. A pure bootstrap should only be treated as a utility ceiling, not a private release.

title: Automated Feature Engineering via Evolutionary Algorithms
recommendation: Use a small evolutionary search when the feature space is rich but hand-engineering is slow. Even a short CPU-only run can surface interactions that linear baselines miss.

title: Imbalanced Data in Graph Neural Networks
recommendation: Before training a full GNN stack, benchmark a graph-aware tabular baseline with neighborhood aggregation. On imbalanced graphs it often captures a large share of the graph signal for a fraction of the engineering cost.

title: Tree-Based Models vs. Noise
recommendation: When data quality is unstable, compare degradation curves rather than only clean-set accuracy. Tree ensembles usually lose performance more gracefully than small neural networks under missingness and label noise.

title: Memory-Safe Feature Extraction Pipelines
recommendation: Use sparse or hashed feature extraction when intrusion logs start to scale. Dense one-hot matrices are simple, but they become the bottleneck long before the classifier does.

title: Algorithmic Optimization of Tree Ensembles
recommendation: Benchmark tree ensembles with both accuracy and systems metrics. ExtraTrees or shallow forests often give a better production trade-off than a raw accuracy leader that is slower to fit or score.

title: Numerical Stability in Label Encoding
recommendation: Avoid low-width wrapped ordinal codes for high-cardinality features. If memory is tight, prefer sparse one-hot or smoothed target encoding rather than forcing categories into a tiny integer range.

title: Security-Aware ML for Intrusion Detection
recommendation: Treat intrusion detection as a cost-sensitive problem. Pick the model and threshold that maximize attack recall without letting precision collapse, rather than optimizing plain accuracy.

title: Deterministic Agentic Data Validation
recommendation: Validate messy operational data before model selection. Rule-driven cleaning often yields larger gains than swapping between two strong regressors on corrupted inputs.
